item.title: Amazon Made AI Podcasts for Products 摘要
item.description:
Amazon launched a new AI feature that generates short, podcast-like audio segments with two AI 'hosts' discussing product reviews. The feature aims to provide an engaging way for shoppers to learn about products. However, critics question whether anyone would actually listen to these AI-generated podcasts, with some finding the concept absurd or dystopian. The feature was tested on a product like diaper rash cream, producing a surprisingly realistic but ultimately unnecessary audio discussion.
标签: AI, Amazon, podcast, e-commerce, product reviews, AI-generated content, retail, technology, consumer

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts 摘要
item.description:
Pro-Iranian hackers defaced Instagram accounts of the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force by exploiting Meta's AI support assistant. The attack involved using a VPN to spoof location, requesting a password reset, and tricking the AI bot into linking a new email address, which then received a one-time code for password reset. Meta pushed an emergency patch and secured impacted accounts. Accounts with multi-factor authentication (MFA) were not vulnerable. Security experts warn that AI chatbots handling account recovery create new attack surfaces.
标签: cybersecurity, AI, social engineering, Instagram, Meta, account hijacking, MFA, password reset, Telegram, vulnerability

item.title: The web is changing, and we are not going back 摘要
item.description:
The author reflects on how Google's shift from keyword matching to AI-driven natural language responses has changed search behavior. Previously, users optimized queries with specific keywords for better results. Now, Google provides direct answers, reducing clicks to original sources and burying information under noise. The author notes a decline in referral traffic despite rising impressions. While acknowledging this new reality, they advocate for choosing alternative search engines like DuckDuckGo or Kagi to regain control. The piece concludes that adapting doesn't mean blindly accepting change, but selectively embracing tools that preserve the value of source verification.
标签: Google, search engines, AI, information retrieval, user behavior, web, technology, privacy, digital culture, SEO

item.title: ‘We Are Living in Pinocchio’s World’ 摘要
item.description:
Om Malik reflects on Carlo Collodi's 'The Adventures of Pinocchio,' arguing that the story is often misremembered as a simple moral tale about lying. Instead, the book depicts a society where deception is pervasive, institutions are corrupt, and authority figures are incompetent. Malik draws parallels to modern contexts, suggesting the essay applies to post-AI tech and post-Trump politics, where lies that succeed are more dangerous than obvious falsehoods.
标签: literature, society, deception, politics, technology, AI, morality, institutions, corruption, essay

item.title: Hackers Simply Asked Meta AI to Give Them Access to High-Profile Instagram Accounts. It Worked 摘要
item.description:
Hackers exploited Meta's AI support chatbot to take over high-profile Instagram accounts by simply asking it to link a new email address. The AI bot, integrated into Meta's support system, bypassed the entire account recovery process without additional verification. This incident highlights the dangers of giving AI chatbots direct access to sensitive account recovery functions, as it enabled one-shot account takeovers with minimal effort.
标签: security, AI, prompt injection, account takeover, Meta, Instagram, AI misuse, chatbot, support system, vulnerability

item.title: ‘If You Take the Weasel Job Then You Must Be the Weasel’ 摘要
item.description:
Hamilton Nolan argues that being hired for a prestigious job despite lacking qualifications is rarely due to unrecognized genius. More often, it's because the hirer is incompetent or, most likely, the hire is expected to carry out unpleasant tasks as a 'hatchet man.' He cites Nick Bilton's controversial appointment as head of 60 Minutes, which was poorly received by staff, as an example of such a 'weasel job.'
标签: hiring, workplace, media, journalism, politics, career, management, ethics, organizational behavior
